data.title: Biggest stock movers Wednesday: HIMS, NKLA, BMBL, GB, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged lower on Wednesday, the day after the S&P 500 closed at a record high, with investors navigating geopolitical and trade uncertainties while awaiting the Federal Reserve's meeting minutes for further policy insights.

Here are some of Wednesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Global Blue (NYSE:GB) shares soared 17% after Shift4 Payments (NYSE:FOUR) announced a $2.5B acquisition deal, offering $7.50 per share in cash, a 15% premium to GB’s last close. The deal, expected to close by Q3, will integrate GB’s tax refund and currency conversion solutions into Shift4’s payments platform. Despite the expansion move, Shift4 shares dropped 9% post-announcement.
  • Appian (APPN) shares surged 24% after Q4 results exceeded expectations, with revenue rising 14.7% Y/Y. Cloud subscription revenue grew 19% Y/Y, while non-GAAP operating income reached $18.7M, reversing a $(1.4)M loss in the prior year, and adjusted EBITDA jumped to $21.2M from $1.0M. For Q1 2025, the company expects cloud subscription revenue of $97M-$99M (+12%-14% Y/Y) and total revenue of $162M-$164M, slightly below the $167M consensus, with adjusted EBITDA of $8M-$10M and non-GAAP EPS of $0.02-$0.05, above the $0.00 consensus. Full-year 2025 guidance includes cloud subscription revenue of $419M-$421M (+14% Y/Y) and total revenue of $680M-$684M (+10% Y/Y), aligning with the $683.09M consensus, while adjusted EBITDA is projected at $38M-$42M and non-GAAP EPS at $0.17-$0.22, topping the $0.12 consensus.
  • Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) shares jumped 16% after announcing plans to introduce at-home blood draws and whole-body testing through its platform following the acquisition of at-home lab testing facility Trybe Labs. The deal, funded with cash on hand, involves acquiring New Jersey-based Sigmund NJ LLC, which operates Trybe Labs’ services. HIMS expects to roll out at-home testing capabilities over the next year, with Chief Medical Officer Patrick Carroll highlighting the move as a step toward more personalized, comprehensive care, enabling providers to leverage richer data for improved clinical decisions

Biggest stock losers

  • Bumble (NASDAQ:BMBL) shares plunged 18% after Q4 results revealed a decline in Total Average Revenue per Paying User (ARPPU) to $20.58 from $22.64 a year ago, with Q1 2025 revenue projected to decline 7%-10% Y/Y to $242M-$248M, falling short of the $255.48M consensus; Bumble App revenue is expected to decrease 6%-8% to $198M-$202M, while adjusted EBITDA is forecasted at $60M-$63M (25% margin), as the company refocuses on product momentum, revenue strategy, and safety enhancements, discontinuing Fruitz and Official, and anticipates a short-term loss of 100K-120K paying users.
  • Arista Networks (NYSE:ANET) shares dropped 4% despite the company delivering better-than-expected Q4 results and guidance, driven by strong AI-related spending. For 1Q25, the company projects revenue between $1.93B and $1.97B, with the $1.95B midpoint exceeding the $1.91B consensus. Adjusted gross margins are expected to be around 63%.
  • Nikola Corporation (NKLA) saw its shares plummet by 45% after voluntarily filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection in the U.S. Bankruptcy Court for the District of Delaware. The company also requested court approval to initiate an auction and sale process under Section 363 of the U.S. Bankruptcy Code. Despite the filing, Nikola plans to maintain limited service and support for its trucks in the field, including certain HYLA fueling operations, until the end of March, after which it will require external partners for continued support. Entering Chapter 11 with approximately $47M in cash, the company aims to fund ongoing operations, facilitate asset sales, and navigate the bankruptcy process efficiently. CEO Steve Girsky acknowledged the challenges faced by the electric vehicle industry, citing efforts to secure capital and stabilize the balance sheet. However, despite these measures, the company determined that Chapter 11 was the most viable option for its future and stakeholders.
  • Etsy (ETSY) shares dropped about 5% after the company reported mixed Q4 results, as weaker holiday spending led to a 6.8% decline in gross merchandise sales (GMS). While the company managed to reactivate 9.8 million lapsed buyers, the number of active buyers fell 2.6%, and spending per buyer declined 3.5% to $121. Despite these challenges, Etsy saw an 8.1% rise in services revenue, which helped offset a 1.4% drop in marketplace revenue, bringing total revenue to $852.2M—up 1.2% Y/Y but missing estimates by $9.2M. Profitability improved as cost of sales fell 15%, leading to an earnings beat of 9 cents per share. Looking ahead, Etsy expects continued GMS weakness in Q1, with declines mirroring the Y/Y trends seen in Q4.

data.title: Garmin jumps to a new all-time high after sailing past earnings expectations on broad sales strength
data.description:
Garmin (NYSE:GRMN) jumped in early trading on Wednesday after revenue jumped 23% year-over-year to $1.82 billion in Q4 to top the expectation of analysts.

The company saw 31% revenue growth in the fitness segment off strong demand for wearables and 29% growth for the outdoor segment on strong demand for adventure watches. Notably, revenue from the auto OEM segment increased 30% during the quarter due to increased shipments of domain controllers.

The increased sales levels helped boost the gross margin rate to 59.3% from 58.3% a year ago and the operating margin to 28.3% of sales from 23.0% a year ago. EPS was reported at $2.41 vs. $2.02 consensus and $1.72 a year ago.

During Q4, Garmin (NYSE:GRMN) generated operating cash flow of $484 million and free cash flow of $399 million. On the balance sheet, Garmin (GRMN) ended the quarter with a cash position of around $3.7 billion.

Looking ahead, Garmin (GRMN) expects full-year revenue of approximately $6.80 billion, an increase of approximately 8% over the level from 2024. GRMN also expects full-year pro forma EPS to be approximately $7.80 based upon gross margin of approximately 58.7%, operating margin of approximately 25.0%, and a pro forma effective tax rate of approximately 16.5%.

"We are entering 2025 with continued strong momentum from our robust product lineup and have many product launches planned during the year," highlighted CEO Cliff Pemble.

Shares of Garmin (GRMN) jumped 11.3% in early trading on Wednesday and swapped hands at a record high of $239.23 earlier in the session.

data.title: DDC Enterprise sees FY24 gross revenue $34M-$40M
data.description:
• DDC Enterprise (NYSE:DDC) anticipates achieving record revenue between $34-$40M with performance between 23.5-27% gross margin, for the calendar year 2024.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for 2024 is expected to be consistent with amounts reported as of June 30, 2024.
  • Cash, equivalents and short-term investments are estimated at $26.2M as of Dec. 31, 2024.
  • On 14 Feb. 2025, DDC announced that it regained compliance with the NYSE continued listing standards set forth in Part 10 of the NYSE American Company Guide.

data.title: Lifeward CEO Larry Jasinski to step down
data.description:
• Lifeward (NASDAQ:LFWD) on Wednesday announced that Larry Jasinski, the company’s chief executive officer, will step down.
  • The company said Jasinski will continue to serve in his existing roles through the second quarter of 2025 to assist with a transition to a successor.
  • The Lifeward Board has appointed a special committee to direct the search for the next CEO and will consider external and internal candidates, in partnership with a nationally recognized firm.
  • The company expects to report revenue for the year ended December 31, 2024, of approximately $25.7 million, aligning with its guidance range of $25 million to $26 million.
  • Additionally, the company expects annual GAAP operating expenses to be approximately $33.4 million, including about $5.7 million in restructuring and impairment expenses.
  • The company plans to provide its financial outlook for 2025 on its fourth-quarter and full-year 2024 financial results conference call to be scheduled during the week of March 10, 2025.
  • LFWD is -2.25% to $1.955.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Shift4 unveils 'very conservative' 2025 guidance on the back of mixed Q4 results; stock tumbles
data.description:
Shift4 Payments (NYSE:FOUR) shares gapped down 13% in Wednesday morning trading after unveiling an annual outlook that missed Wall Street expectations and Q4 2024 results that came in mixed.

The provider of software and payment processing solutions introduced full-year 2025 guidance for volume of $200B-$220B (midpoint $210B trails the $216.1B Visible Alpha consensus). Also for the year, gross revenue less network fees is expected to be $1.65B-$1.72B (midpoint $1.69B falls short of the $1.70B Visible Alpha estimate), and adjusted EBITDA is anticipated to be $830M-$855M (midpoint $842.5M vs. $846.6M Visible Alpha estimate).

Calling the guidance "very conservative," BTIG analyst Andrew Harte reiterated his Buy rating on Shift4 (NYSE:FOUR) shares, as the outlook entails at least 20% organic growth, while the medium-term FY27 outlook entails a high-teens organic" compound annual growth rate, he wrote in a note to clients.

Meanwhile, Q4 2024 adjusted EPS of $1.35, topping the $1.15 average analyst estimate, climbed from $1.04 in Q3 and $0.76 a year earlier.

Q4 gross revenue of $887.0M, below the $946.5M consensus, fell from $909.2M in the prior quarter and rose from $705.4M in Q4 2023.

End-to-end payment volume advanced to $47.9B from $43.5B in Q3 and $32.1B in the year-ago quarter.

Adjusted EBITDA was $205.9M, compared with $187.4M in Q3 and $187.4M in Q4 2023.

"FOUR’s sizeable 4Q24 revenue miss, combined with its soft FY25 guidance, coming at a time when its founder and longtime CEO is in the process of departing, gives us pause," Benchmark's Mark Palmer wrote in a note, reterating his Hold rating on the stock.

Shift4 (FOUR) separately said Tuesday it agreed to acquire tax-free shopping platform Global Blue Holding (GB) for $7.50 per share in cash.

data.title: Pioneer Power expects Q4 revenue of $9.8 million
data.description:
• Pioneer Power Solutions (NASDAQ:PPSI) on Wednesday reported preliminary unaudited Q4 revenue of $9.8 million, compared to consensus revenue estimates of $8.60 million.
  • Preliminary Q4 gross profit of $2.8 million, or a gross margin of 29%, as compared to $610,000, or a gross margin of 23%, for the same quarter in 2023.
  • Preliminary Q4 operating income, excluding R&D expense and non-recurring professional fees, of $1.5 million, as compared to a loss of $66,000 for the same quarter in 2023, a year-over-year improvement of $1.6 million.
  • Preliminary FY revenue of $22.9 million, as compared to $11.1 million for the year ended December 31, 2023, an increase of 106%.
  • Preliminary FY gross profit of $5.5 million, or a gross margin of 24%, as compared to $2.2 million, or a gross margin of 20%, for the year ended December 31, 2023.
  • Preliminary FY operating income, excluding R&D expense and non-recurring professional fees, of $1.6 million, as compared to a loss of $1.3 million for the year ended December 31, 2023, a year-over-year improvement of $2.9 million.
  • Cash on hand at December 31, 2024, was $41.6 million, as compared to $3.6 million at December 31, 2023, an increase of approximately $38.0 million.
  • 2025 Outlook: The company reiterates its expectation for revenue of $27 to $29 million for the full year of 2025.
  • PPSI is +6.38% to $4.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Onar intends to buy marketing tech company Storia
data.description:
• Onar (ONAR) signed a letter of intent to acquire a 100% stake in a marketing technology company that will be merged with its subsidiary, Storia.
  • This acquisition is expected to double Storia's revenue and it aligns with ONAR's mission to expand its network that service middle-market companies.
  • The transaction is expected to close in Q1 2025, subject to due diligence, regulatory approvals, and a definitive agreement.

data.title: Beeline secures $5M funding as CEO invests $2.9M to drive AI mortgage disruption
data.description:
• Eastside Distilling (NASDAQ:BLNE) has successfully closed a $5M private placement, with over half of the capital coming directly from its CEO, highlighting unwavering confidence in the Co.'s vision.
  • The funding will be strategically deployed to accelerate growth, reduce debt, and expand Beeline's AI-driven, low-cost mortgage origination platform.
  • Additionally, the investment will fuel the rapid development of its wholly-owned subsidiary, Beeline Labs, which operates a SaaS business and is transforming mortgage quality control and compliance with cutting-edge automation.
  • "Our AI-driven solutions are gaining rapid adoption, and this investment positions us to scale faster than ever before." said Founder & CEO of Beeline Financial Holdings, Nick Liuzza.
  • With this momentum, Beeline will provide a comprehensive update on its platform-wide advancements and growth trajectory in its upcoming Q4 2024 earnings report next month.

data.title: Nvidia's Blackwell demand remains unfazed by DeepSeek situation: Wedbush
data.description:
Demand for Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) Blackwell GPUs remains much stronger than supply despite the recent DeepSeek noise, according to Wedbush analysts.

"We believe demand is far outstripping supply with Blackwell in the field and after speaking with many enterprise AI customers, we have seen NOT ONE AI enterprise deployment slow down or change due to the DeepSeek situation," said Wedbush analysts, led by Daniel Ives, in a note following a quarterly trip to Asia and a supply chain check. "No customer wants to 'lose their place in line' as it is described to us for Nvidia's next gen chips."

Wedbush estimates that about 10% to 15% of IT budgets will go to artificial intelligence in 2025. Capital expenditures from Magnificent Seven companies will increase about $100B to $325B in 2025 as well.

"We are seeing many IT departments focused on foundational hyperscale deployments for AI around Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), and Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) with a laser focused on the software-driven use cases currently underway," Ives added. "The China trade tensions are a lingering concern to the market, but we do not see this geopolitical poker game with the 202 area code getting in the way of the AI Revolution and DeepSeek if anything has accelerated demand for more companies heading down the AI paths in 2025...not the opposite in our view despite many tech bears coming out of hibernation mode with the DeepSeek soap opera."
